description: Agience Transform — a named, executable transformation step invokable via POST /artifacts/{id}/invoke. For run.type=mcp-tool, dispatch resolves the server via a relationship edge (@relationship.server) and the tool from context.run.tool. For run.type in {workflow, llm, transform-ref}, dispatch falls through to the orchestrator relationship edge (@relationship.orchestrator) which runs execute_transform.

transform.description: Transform identity and dispatch profile. Keep the MIME stable as application/vnd.agience.transform+json and use transform.kind / transform.subtype for sub-types instead of creating new transform MIME families.

kind.description: Semantic transform family used for UX, grouping, and validation. Examples: palette, llm, workflow, script, retrieval, ingest.

subtype.description: Optional narrower subtype within the semantic family. Examples: summarize, classify, research, transcript-finalize.

run.description: Dispatch configuration for this Transform. Defines how the transform executes, independent of the semantic family.

type.description: Execution runner. Examples: palette-run for Palette-authored transform specs, workflow for multi-step orchestrated pipelines, llm for direct prompt execution, host-script for certified host-side script execution.

spec.description: Optional embedded transform spec snapshot, typically used by Palette-authored transforms.

title.description: Human-readable name shown in the Palette

description.description: Brief explanation of what this Transform does

run.description: Execution interface — defines how the Transform is dispatched when invoked via transform_id

type.description: Dispatch mode. 'mcp-tool' calls a named tool on an MCP server; 'workflow' orchestrates a sequence of child transforms with conditions and retry; 'llm' runs a prompt; 'transform-ref' delegates to another Transform artifact; 'webhook' POSTs to a URL.

server.description: DEPRECATED — server relationship is now defined by an edge, not a context field. Retained for schema compatibility during migration.

tool.description: Tool name to invoke on the MCP server (type=mcp-tool only)

input_mapping.description: Maps tool parameter names → values sourced from the invoke request. String values starting with '$.' are resolved as paths: '$.workspace_id', '$.artifacts[0]', etc.

transform_id.description: Artifact ID of another Transform to delegate to (type=transform-ref only)

state_type.description: Content type of the State Artifact (type=workflow only, informational)

steps.description: Ordered list of child transform steps (type=workflow only)

transform_id.description: Artifact ID of the child Transform to execute

condition.description: Optional condition evaluated against State Artifact context. Step is skipped if condition is not met.

input_mapping.description: Additional input_mapping overrides for this step

on_field.description: Field in State Artifact context to check for retry trigger

restart_from_step.description: 0-based index of the step to restart from on retry

input.description: Declares what the Transform expects as input (for documentation and palette display)

output.description: Declares what the Transform produces (for documentation and palette display)

drop.description: Drop zone configuration. When enabled, the artifact card accepts artifact drops and invokes the transform with the dropped artifact IDs.

enabled.description: Whether this transform card accepts artifact drops. Default false.

label.description: Tooltip shown when dragging over the card. Default: 'Drop to run'.

accepts.description: MIME content_type filters the card will accept. Supports wildcards (e.g. 'text/*'). Omit or set ['*/*'] to accept any artifact.
